issues.title: API: add context-manager support to AlmaAPIClient (with-statement)

tests.scope: Exercise the with-statement happy path against live SANDBOX. Build an AlmaAPIClient inside a `with` block, perform one real read against an existing user, then verify the session is torn down cleanly on block exit (the documented post-close sentinel: `_session is None`). Covers AC #13.1 (`with AlmaAPIClient(...) as alma:` works) and AC #13.4 (test exercising the with-statement path) end-to-end with a live HTTP call rather than a mocked response.

needsHumanInput.description: An existing user_primary_id in SANDBOX that can be safely fetched read-only. Used purely to drive one real GET inside the `with` block so the context-manager path is exercised against live HTTP. Synthetic placeholder only — never a real operator-supplied identifier.

pythonCalls:
with AlmaAPIClient(environment='SANDBOX') as alma:
    captured_client = alma
    users = Users(alma)
    captured_response = users.get_user('${existing_user_primary_id}')

tests.scope: Verify that calling any HTTP verb on an AlmaAPIClient instance after `close()` raises a clear AlmaAPIError (the documented behaviour for #13 — pick raise-on-use-after-close). Build the client, make one successful read against SANDBOX to confirm it is wired up correctly, call `close()` twice to confirm idempotency, then attempt another GET and verify the typed error. Covers AC #13.2 (idempotent close) and AC #13.3 (post-close HTTP raises a clear error) under live conditions.

needsHumanInput.description: An existing user_primary_id in SANDBOX that can be safely fetched read-only. Used purely to drive one real GET inside the `with` block so the context-manager path is exercised against live HTTP. Synthetic placeholder only — never a real operator-supplied identifier.

issues.title: Quality: tighten exception handling and cache AlmaResponse.data

unmappable.reason: Source-tree invariant — verified by static inspection / a guard test in tests/unit/client/test_alma_response.py (and trivially by `grep -n 'except:' src/almaapitk/client/`). No Alma API call would observe the absence of a bare except clause.

unmappable.ac: `AlmaResponse._response.json()` is called at most once per response across `.json()`, `.data`, and the debug-body-logging path.
unmappable.reason: Internal call-count invariant on a private attribute. SANDBOX returns identical bytes regardless of how many times the wrapper parses them — observable only by patching `requests.Response.json` with a counter, which is what the unit tests in tests/unit/client/test_alma_response.py already do.

unmappable.ac: `_safe_response_body` (or equivalent) is the single place that decides whether to call `response.json()`.
unmappable.reason: Module-structure invariant — verified by code review and the unit tests (which assert that `AlmaResponse._safe_body` and the error-extraction path both route through the cached body). Not observable via any SANDBOX endpoint.

unmappable.ac: A unit test asserts `.data` is parsed exactly once even when accessed multiple times.
unmappable.reason: Explicitly a unit-test deliverable — covered by the new tests in tests/unit/client/test_alma_response.py with a mocked Response whose `.json()` is a counter. SANDBOX cannot observe parse counts on the client-side wrapper.
